title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Browser and Node Client Systems · Inference Brief: Private Shared Cache
summary: An offline-first dashboard combines browser HTTP caching, a service-worker cache, ETags, Vary, and user-specific responses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts private shared cache increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch spec-aware cache decision notebook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Inject short-lived credentials through a testable provider with rotation, concurrency, redaction, and failure policy. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Private Shared Cache · Theory Private Shared Cache
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model stores a response with max-age=50, ETag "rev-5", Age:5, and Vary: Accept-Language. Competency probe: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
